Name: Sprigatito
Flavor-Text: The sweet scent its body gives off mesmerizes those around it. The scent grows stronger when this Pokémon is in the sun.

Name: Floragato
Flavor-Text: The hardness of Floragato’s fur depends on the Pokémon’s mood. When Floragato is prepared to battle, its fur becomes pointed and needle sharp.

Name: Meowscarada ex
Effects: Choose a spot from among your opponent's Active Spot and Bench. At the end of your opponent's next turn, do 70 damage to the Pokémon in the spot you chose.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Tarountula
Flavor-Text: The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread’s strength is the topic of ongoing research.

Name: Spidops
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text: Spidops covers its territory in tough, sticky threads to set up traps for intruders.

Name: Nymble
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s highly skilled at a fighting style in which it uses its jumping capabilities to dodge incoming attacks while also dealing damage to opponents.

Name: Smoliv
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

Name: Dolliv
Flavor-Text: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

Name: Arboliva
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon ex that has any Energy attached. If you do, discard a random Energy from that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This calm Pokémon is very compassionate. It will share its delicious, nutrient-rich oil with weakened Pokémon.

Name: Bramblin
Flavor-Text: Not even Bramblin knows where it is headed as it tumbles across the wilderness, blown by the wind. It loathes getting wet.

Name: Brambleghast
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Brambleghast wanders around arid regions. On rare occasions, mass outbreaks of these Pokémon will bury an entire town.

Name: Capsakid
Flavor-Text: Traditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy because they include the shed front teeth of Capsakid among their ingredients.

Name: Scovillain
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a [G] or [M] Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The green head has turned vicious due to the spicy chemicals stimulating its brain. Once it goes on a rampage, there is no stopping it.

Name: Rellor
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It rolls its mud ball around while the energy it needs for evolution matures. Eventually the time comes for it to evolve.

Name: Wo-Chien
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon cost 2 [C] more.
Flavor-Text: It drains the life-force from vegetation, causing nearby forests to instantly wither and fields to turn barren.

Name: Fuecoco
Effects: Put 1 random Basic Pokémon from your deck onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: Its flame sac is small, so energy is always leaking out. This energy is released from the dent atop Fuecoco’s head and flickers to and fro.

Name: Crocalor
Flavor-Text: The valve in Crocalor’s flame sac is closely connected to its vocal cords. This Pokémon utters a guttural cry as it spews flames every which way.

Name: Skeledirge
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may discard 1 [R] Energy from this Pokémon in order to use this Ability. During this turn, attacks used by your [R] Pokémon do +50 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Skeledirge’s gentle singing soothes the souls of all that hear it. It burns its enemies to a crisp with flames of over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Charcadet
Flavor-Text: Its firepower increases when it fights, reaching over 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit. It likes berries that are rich in fat.

Name: Armarouge ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Chi-Yu
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The envy accumulated within curved beads that sparked multiple conflicts has clad itself in fire and become a Pokémon.

Name: Quaxly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its strong legs let it easily swim around in even fast-flowing rivers. It likes to keep things tidy and is prone to overthinking things.

Name: Quaxwell
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The hardworking Quaxwell observes people and Pokémon from various regions and incorporates their movements into its own dance routines.

Name: Quaquaval
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon's remaining HP is 50 or less, attacks used by this Pokémon do +60 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Dancing in ways that evoke far-away places, this Pokémon mesmerizes all that see it. Flourishes of its decorative water feathers slice into its foes.

Name: Wiglett
Flavor-Text: Though it looks like Diglett, Wiglett is an entirely different species. The resemblance seems to be a coincidental result of environmental adaptation.

Name: Wugtrio
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: A variety of fish Pokémon, Wugtrio was once considered to be a regional form of Dugtrio.

Name: Finizen
Flavor-Text: Its water ring is made from seawater mixed with a sticky fluid that Finizen secretes from its blowhole.

Name: Palafin
Effects: This attack also does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This hero of the ocean swims at a speed of 50 knots and saves drowning people and Pokémon.

Name: Cetoddle
Flavor-Text: It lives in frigid regions in pods of five or so individuals. It loves the minerals found in snow and ice.

Name: Cetitan
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Ice energy builds up in the horn on its upper jaw, causing the horn to reach cryogenic temperatures that freeze its surroundings.

Name: Veluza
Effects: If you have no cards in your deck, this attack can be used for 1 [W] Energy.
Flavor-Text: Veluza has excellent regenerative capabilities. It sheds spare flesh from its body to boost its agility, then charges at its prey.

Name: Dondozo
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.
Flavor-Text: It treats Tatsugiri like its boss and follows it loyally. Though powerful, Dondozo is apparently not very smart.

Name: Tatsugiri
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Tatsugiri is an extremely cunning Pokémon. It feigns weakness to lure in prey, then orders its partner to attack.

Name: Frigibax
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in forests and craggy areas. Using the power of its dorsal fin, it cools the inside of its nest like a refrigerator.

Name: Arctibax
Flavor-Text: It attacks with the blade of its frozen dorsal fin by doing a front flip in the air. Arctibax’s strong back and legs allow it to pull off this technique.

Name: Baxcalibur
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to the [W] Pokémon in the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: It launches itself into battle by flipping upside down and spewing frigid air from its mouth. It finishes opponents off with its dorsal blade.

Name: Chien-Pao ex
Effects: Discard all [W] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 130 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Pawmi
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

Name: Pawmo
Flavor-Text: Pawmo uses a unique fighting technique in which it uses its forepaws to strike foes and zap them with electricity from its paw pads simultaneously.

Name: Pawmot
Effects: Discard a [L] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Pawmot’s fluffy fur acts as a battery. It can store the same amount of electricity as an electric car.

Name: Tadbulb
Flavor-Text: It floats using the electricity stored in its body. When thunderclouds are around, Tadbulb will float higher off the ground.

Name: Bellibolt ex
Effects: If you have 4 or more [L] Energy in play, this attack does 70 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Wattrel
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon make their nests on coastal cliffs. The nests have a strange, crackling texture, and they’re a popular delicacy.

Name: Kilowattrel
Flavor-Text: It uses its throat sac to store electricity generated by its wings. There’s hardly any oil in its feathers, so it is a poor swimmer.

Name: Miraidon
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This seems to be the Iron Serpent mentioned in an old book. The Iron Serpent is said to have turned the land to ash with its lightning.

Name: Fidough
Flavor-Text: The yeast in Fidough’s breath is useful for cooking, so this Pokémon has been protected by people since long ago.

Name: Dachsbun
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The pleasant aroma that emanates from this Pokémon’s body helps wheat grow, so Dachsbun has been treasured by farming villages.

Name: Ceruledge
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: An old set of armor steeped in grudges caused this Pokémon’s evolution. Ceruledge cuts its enemies to pieces without mercy.

Name: Rabsca
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: An infant sleeps inside the ball. Rabsca rolls the ball soothingly with its legs to ensure the infant sleeps comfortably.

Name: Flittle
Flavor-Text: It spends its time running around wastelands. If anyone steals its beloved berries, it will chase them down and exact its revenge.

Name: Espathra
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It emits psychic power from the gaps between its multicolored frills and sprints at speeds greater than 120 mph.

Name: Greavard
Effects: Discard a card from your hand. If you can't, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This friendly Pokémon doesn’t like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

Name: Houndstone
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [P] Pokémon in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: A lovingly mourned Pokémon was reborn as Houndstone. It doesn’t like anyone touching the protuberance atop its head.

Name: Gimmighoul
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It lives inside an old treasure chest. Sometimes it gets left in shop corners since no one realizes it’s actually a Pokémon.

Name: Mankey
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It is extremely quick to anger. It could be docile one moment, then thrashing away the next instant.

Name: Primeape
Effects: This attack does more damage equal to the damage this Pokémon has on it.
Flavor-Text: Some researchers theorize that Primeape remains angry even when inside a Poké Ball.

Name: Annihilape
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It imbues its fists with the power of the rage that it kept hidden in its heart. Opponents struck by these imbued fists will be shattered to their core.

Name: Paldean Tauros
Flavor-Text: This kind of Tauros, known as the Combat Breed, is distinguished by its thick, powerful muscles and its fierce disposition.

Name: Toedscool
Flavor-Text: Toedscool lives in muggy forests. The flaps that fall from its body are chewy and very delicious.

Name: Toedscruel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It coils its 10 tentacles around prey and sucks out their nutrients, causing the prey pain. The folds along the rim of its head are a popular delicacy.

Name: Klawf
Effects: This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives on sheer cliffs. It sidesteps opponents’ attacks, then lunges for their weak spots with its claws.

Name: Ting-Lu
Effects: If this Pokémon's remaining HP is 60 or less, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It slowly brings its exceedingly heavy head down upon the ground, splitting the earth open with huge fissures that run over 160 feet deep.

Name: Koraidon
Effects: Discard 2 [F] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon resembles Cyclizar, but it is far burlier and more ferocious. Nothing is known about its ecology or other features.

Name: Paldean Wooper
Flavor-Text: It’s dangerous for Wooper to travel alone. They line up in groups of three or four and help each other as they walk around the wetlands.

Name: Paldean Clodsire
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.
Flavor-Text: It lives at the bottom of ponds and swamps. It will carry Wooper on its back and ferry them across water from one shore to the other.

Name: Lokix
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It uses its normally folded third set of legs when in Showdown Mode. This places a huge burden on its body, so it can’t stay in this mode for long.

Name: Maschiff
Flavor-Text: Its well-developed jaw and fangs are strong enough to crunch through boulders, and its thick fat makes for an excellent defense.

Name: Mabosstiff
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Mabosstiff loves playing with children. Though usually gentle, it takes on an intimidating look when protecting its family.

Name: Shroodle
Flavor-Text: To keep enemies away from its territory, it paints markings around its nest using a poisonous liquid that has an acrid odor.

Name: Grafaiai
Effects: If your Pokémon in play have 3 or more different types of Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Each Grafaiai paints its own individual pattern, and it will paint that same pattern over and over again throughout its life.

Name: Bombirdier
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.

Name: Tinkatink
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon pounds iron scraps together to make a hammer. It will remake the hammer again and again until it’s satisfied with the result.

Name: Tinkatuff
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon make their homes in piles of scrap metal. They test the strength of each other’s hammers by smashing them together.

Name: Tinkaton
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Gigaton Hammer.
Flavor-Text: The hammer tops 220 pounds, yet it gets swung around easily by Tinkaton as it steals whatever it pleases and carries its plunder back home.

Name: Varoom
Flavor-Text: The steel section is Varoom’s actual body. This Pokémon clings to rocks and converts the minerals within into energy to fuel its activities.

Name: Revavroom
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Revavroom viciously threatens others with the sound of its exhaust. It sticks its tongue out from its cylindrical mouth and sprays toxic fluids.

Name: Orthworm
Effects: Take a [M] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in arid deserts. It maintains its metal body by consuming iron from the soil.

Name: Gholdengo ex
Effects: 1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon. For each time a Pokémon was chosen, do 40 damage to it.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Lechonk
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon spurns all but the finest of foods. Its body gives off an herblike scent that bug Pokémon detest.

Name: Oinkologne
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.
Flavor-Text: It entrances female Pokémon with the sweet, alluring scent that wafts from all over its body.

Name: Tandemaus
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The pair sticks together no matter what. They split any food they find exactly in half and then eat it together.

Name: Maushold
Effects: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The little one just appeared one day. They all live together like a family, but the relationship between the three is still unclear.

Name: Squawkabilly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Green-feathered flocks hold the most sway. When they’re out searching for food in the mornings and evenings, it gets very noisy.

Name: Cyclizar
Effects: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It can sprint at over 70 mph while carrying a human. The rider’s body heat warms Cyclizar’s back and lifts the Pokémon’s spirit.

Name: Flamigo
Flavor-Text: Thanks to a behavior of theirs known as “synchronizing,” an entire flock of these Pokémon can attack simultaneously in perfect harmony.

Name: Electric Generator
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched [L] Pokémon.

Name: Big Air Balloon
Description: The Stage 2 Pokémon this card is attached to has no Retreat Cost.

Name: Team Star Grunts
Description: Discard a random Energy from among the Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon that have an Ability.

Name: Iono
Description: Each player shuffles the cards in their hand into their deck, then draws that many cards.

Name: Nemona
Description: During this turn, attacks used by your Pawmot do +80 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex.

Name: Arven
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, put a random Item card from your deck into your hand. If tails, put a random Pokémon Tool card from your deck into your hand.

Name: Penny
Description: Look at a random Supporter card that's not Penny from your opponent's deck and shuffle it back into their deck. Use the effect of that card as the effect of this card.

Name: Mesagoza
Description: Once during each player's turn, that player may flip a coin. If heads, that player puts a random Pokémon from their deck into their hand.

Name: Fuecoco
Effects: Put 1 random Basic Pokémon from your deck onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: Its flame sac is small, so energy is always leaking out. This energy is released from the dent atop Fuecoco’s head and flickers to and fro.

Effects: Discard a card from your hand. If you can't, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This friendly Pokémon doesn’t like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in arid deserts. It maintains its metal body by consuming iron from the soil.

Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Effects: If you have 4 or more [L] Energy in play, this attack does 70 more damage.

Effects: 1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon. For each time a Pokémon was chosen, do 40 damage to it.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Arven
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, put a random Item card from your deck into your hand. If tails, put a random Pokémon Tool card from your deck into your hand.

Name: Sprigatito
Flavor-Text: The sweet scent its body gives off mesmerizes those around it. The scent grows stronger when this Pokémon is in the sun.

Name: Floragato
Flavor-Text: The hardness of Floragato’s fur depends on the Pokémon’s mood. When Floragato is prepared to battle, its fur becomes pointed and needle sharp.

Name: Meowscarada
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.

Name: Pawmi
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

Name: Pawmo
Flavor-Text: Pawmo uses a unique fighting technique in which it uses its forepaws to strike foes and zap them with electricity from its paw pads simultaneously.

Name: Pawmot
Effects: Discard a [L] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Pawmot’s fluffy fur acts as a battery. It can store the same amount of electricity as an electric car.

Name: Gimmighoul
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It lives inside an old treasure chest. Sometimes it gets left in shop corners since no one realizes it’s actually a Pokémon.

Name: Tinkatink
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon pounds iron scraps together to make a hammer. It will remake the hammer again and again until it’s satisfied with the result.

Name: Tinkatuff
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon make their homes in piles of scrap metal. They test the strength of each other’s hammers by smashing them together.

Name: Gholdengo
Effects: Flip a coin for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its body seems to be made up of 1,000 coins. This Pokémon gets along well with others and is quick to make friends with anybody.

Name: Entei ex
Ability-Effect: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw a card.
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [R] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Suicune ex
Ability-Effect: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw a card.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Raikou ex
Ability-Effect: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw a card.
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Tinkaton ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Baxcalibur
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to the [W] Pokémon in the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: It launches itself into battle by flipping upside down and spewing frigid air from its mouth. It finishes opponents off with its dorsal blade.

Name: Electric Generator
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched [L] Pokémon.
